page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs based on the Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32 physical cores with 1 thread per core, 64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core, and a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent, and single-threaded gzip compression rated in the bottom 10 percent. However, the instance leverages its 32 dedicated cores to deliver average multi-core performance. Multi-threaded benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core, consistently rank in the middle 50 percent. Memory bandwidth is also average, with uncached read speeds at 98.09 GB/sec and write speeds at 43.54 GB/sec, though the overall Passmark memory score is weak.

The ecs.c5.8xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from parallel execution rather than raw single-threaded speed. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded processing tasks, such as software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving, where resource density can be maximized. However, the lack of local storage and lower memory-to-vCPU ratio make it less suitable for memory-heavy databases or high-I/O storage applications. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and moderate LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and provides a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core performance but average multi-core capabilities, with multi-threaded stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench scores placing in the middle 50 percent. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory density or local disk I/O. It is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core compute-optimized instance designed for parallelized workloads, multi-threaded processing, and high-throughput web serving.

10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel processing
Weak single-core performance and low memory-to-vCPU ratio
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and batch processing
